title: Level 700
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 700: The Lunarscape}}
{{Background|file=moonbgfi2.png}}
{{Blurbackground|file=200moonbg2.jpg}}
{{Audio|mono=yes|align=left|file=Moonnoise.mp3|volume=0.1|=yes|loop=yes|secrettext=LUNA}} {{Audio|mono=yes|align=left|file=QuindarTone.mp3|volume=0.1|=yes|loop=yes|secrettext=LUNA}}
[[File:Blackestmoon.webp|link=|981px]]
Do you remember back when you were in the Frontrooms?
You'd look at the beautiful night sky, adorned with stars, bands, and all types of wonders!
But what truly, truly lit up your nights sitting there, was '''the Moon'''.
Everyone had a different interpretation. The shadow to the sun, the goddess who would come and light up the darkness.
Curious? You were. ''We'' were.
How would it feel if you could be there? Standing atop the very ball of light that you'd go out to see every night.
It was but a dream. A reach, yet too far for humanity.
But now, you can finally see it for yourself. ''Feel'' it for yourself.
Except, it's no longer one giant leap for mankind.
But another plunge into the hell of the Backrooms.
Level 700:
THE LUNARSCAPE
⏾
{{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Void.png|hexagon=☾|entity=Dangerous Phenomena|text-color=white|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|class=4}}
  ✎ '''Overview'''
{{Blurimage|file=Blackestmoon.webp|caption=The clearest photograph of Level 700, with the Dusk Star visible at the left side.|align=right}} '''Level 700''', known as '''The Lunarscape''', is a barren rocky cratered expanse resembling the Moon, suspended in a void akin to outer space. The level exhibits low gravity, rendering Wanderers nearly weightless. Despite its visual similarity to the Moon, Level 700 possesses anomalous properties. The terrain primarily consists of Moonstone, a rock resembling igneous material. Moonstone has applications in medicine, construction, and occasionally as a light source under specific conditions. While oxygen is present, it is scarce, and the inhaling of Moonstone dust can heavily affect the lungs, rendering the environment unsuitable for long-term habitation. The level is cold and hazardous without proper equipment and leaving its surface is impossible. It is illuminated by a celestial body known as the Dusk Star. Due to its hostile environment and lack of resources, Level 700 is classified as a dangerous level and should only be entered with caution and preparation.
  ✎ '''Formations & Structures'''
{{Blurimage|file=Mooncave.png|caption=The inside of a cave.}} Level 700's formations include mounds, hills, caves, and, less frequently, arches. Rarer formations include snowy terrains, frozen "plants" and astronaut footprints. While most structures are empty, some caves contain Moonstone mines, suggesting the possibility of pre-discovery settlements. Researchers have observed formations suggesting a former presence of water, such as dried streambeds and canyons, however, few water sources have been discovered. Some formations deviate entirely from Frontrooms geology, with rare reports of entire ruined office spaces—resembling those found in [[Level 4]], or parking zones similar to [[Level 1]]—embedded deep within cave systems. Strangely enough, these "formations" don't have any connection to the levels they resemble. Spatial consistency within the level is unreliable. Wanderers have reported exiting caves into completely different locations, or passing under arches to find areas being different from what they were. These anomalies are attributed to the level’s instability. {{Blurimage|file=Sublunalakes.png|caption=A sublunarian lake.}} ==Sublunarian Water Bodies== Sublunarian Water Bodies are subterranean formations beneath Level 700 and represent the level’s only known source of water. The water is highly hazardous and unfit for consumption due to the presence of microscopic Moonstone fragments, which are harmful upon ingestion or contact. These bodies of water range from small puddles to expansive lakes and occasionally fully submerged chambers. Visibility is minimal, as most areas are engulfed in complete darkness, increasing the risk of injury or disorientation. However, Glowing Moonstone and other valuable resources are more abundant in these submerged regions. As a result, [[M.E.G.]] routinely deploys specialized teams to survey and extract materials from these zones despite the dangers.{{Blurimage|file=Lunavillage.webp|caption=A deserted town.|align=left}} ==Deserted Towns== Deserted Towns, or Luna Towns, are rare abandoned settlements scattered across Level 700. These towns consist of small, decaying buildings made out of Moonstone no taller than 25 feet in height, often obstructed by rubble and debris. Structures such as broken streetlights and rusted bus stops are common, yet no evidence of prior inhabitants has been found. Occasionally, chests can be discovered within these buildings, typically containing Moonstone, with rarer finds including [[Object 1|Almond Water]] or [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]. Notably, Deserted Towns exhibit spatial instability; moving far enough from them may cause the entire settlement to vanish without trace, for reasons currently unknown. Deserted Towns are more likely to be completely destroyed upon finding, and they are not reliable to search or loot should one be looking for resources. While offering no long-term utility, these towns can provide temporary shelter from the cold, serving as brief refuges for Wanderers traversing the level.
  ✎ '''Anomalous Presence'''
{{Blurimage|file=Moonstro.png|caption=The "Astronaut" phenomenon. Area around phenomenon highlighted.}} The existence of entities in Level 700 remains unconfirmed and is a topic of ongoing debate. Evidence such as fossilized remains and unexplained astronaut footprints suggests prior entity activity, yet no entities have been directly encountered. A recurring phenomenon, often misidentified as an entity, is known as the Astronaut phenomenon. It typically occurs during the level’s nighttime phase and manifests as a distant, silhouetted figure resembling an astronaut. These figures may appear partially obscured—behind rocks, in shadows, or in open view—but always vanish when approached. Usually, after the phenomenon, the victim will enter a state of paranoia and dread, and the phenomenon might happen more often. The origins and causes of the Astronaut phenomenon are unknown, though most evidence suggests it might be a perceptive phenomenon that occurs after staying in the level for prolonged times. ==The Dusk Star== {{Blurimage|file=Moonight.png|caption=Nighttime in Level 700.}} '''The Dusk Star''' is a object seen suspended in the void of Level 700. It acts similar to the Sun, illuminating the level. The Dusk Star may disappear sometimes, leaving the level in pure darkness. The Dusk Star, through unknown means, can have phases. While it can be helpful in illuminating Level 700, it also has anomalous properties that pose a threat to Wanderers. The Dusk Star, while visually harmless, is associated with a hazardous anomaly known as the Nightgaze phenomenon. When a Wanderer looks directly at the Dusk Star, they may become immobilized and unresponsive. Attempts to interact with or assist the affected individual are ineffective. After a brief period, the individual disappears entirely. Their fate remains unknown, and recovery efforts have consistently failed. As such, those affected are presumed deceased. Research indicates the phenomenon is triggered by a light-sensitive response in the eye activated by the Dusk Star’s illumination. This risk persists throughout daylight hours, with exposure increasing likelihood of activation. The phenomenon appears random, but is more likely when direct visual contact with the star occurs. During daytime, remaining in shadow is the most reliable method of prevention. Additionally, drinking Almond Water can temporarily make you immune to this phenomenon.
  ✎ '''Moonstone'''
{{Blurimage|file=Moonrock00.jpg|caption=A sample of Moonstone kept in [[Base Alpha]].}} '''Moonstone''' is a igneous rock native and exclusive to Level 700 that can be used for multiple purposes. Moonstone can be found across all of Level 700, with different sizes and shapes. Moonstone can be melted and refined to produce various medicines, used to treat the effects of Carpetfluid, [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]], and [[Object 44|Cashew Water]]. However, overdosing can cause intoxication and may be fatal. Moonstone also serves as a plentiful and effective source of combustible material, and can be combined with [[Object 15|Firesalt]] to create efficient weaponry. ==Glowing Moonstone== '''Glowing Moonstone''' is a rarer variant of Moonstone that glows a whitish-blue. While near-impossible to find on the surface, Glowing Moonstone can be found more commonly in deeper formations such as caves, burrows and mines. Glowing Moonstone isn't as effective as regular Moonstone for general usage, however, it is a key resource used for creating [[Object 26|Moonlanterns]]. It may also be used for other standard light sources such as lightbulbs or lamps.
  ✎ '''Settlements'''
Due to the hazardous nature of this level, colonies & outposts are impossible to create.
  ✎ '''Entrances & Exits'''
Entrances *Floating far enough into the vacuum space in [[Level 24]] will cause you to land on Level 700 after a period of time. *Trying to live under the moonlight in [[Level 246]] will sometimes take you here. Exits *Nearly drowning in any Sublunarian Water Body will take you all the way to [[Level 7]]. *Survive the Eclipse, and you will be sent to [[Level 410]].

|body=
[[File:Reddestmoon.png|link=|981px]]
"..Am I honest with you?"
"What?"
"I don't think going this is a good idea."
"No shit. It's a class 4 level."
"No no no, it's not that. Look- there's a lot of ways you can die there. Easily. Either you get turned into a statue facing upward, get trapped in a endless cave, or..."
"Or what?"
"...I've been getting many reports about some new "phenomenon" happening in the level. Apparently, if your luck is bad enough, the entire level turns red and you basically die."
"..Yeah. Another one of those edgy phenomena the Backrooms has. What gives? I don't wanna come off as nonchalant, but we've been through worse. I mean, have we documented what this "red" thing is?"
"I don't know. I don't even know if there's a page for this level yet-"
"Then the least we can do is help document it, don't you think?"
"No. I don't think it's worth it, man."
"Fine. It's okay. You can stay back here. But I'm going."
"WHAT!? That's even WORSE. You can't just go out alone!"
"High risk, high reward. It's the mentality one needs if the wanna get through this hellhole."
"...I won't stop you, if so. Just know that in all truth, none of us know what we're doing."
"You're.. right." ''sigh'' "..Don't worry. I'll be back. Promise."

[[File:APOLLOINFINITE.png|left|thumb|125px|link=Apollo-∞]]
'''Exploration Agent Reported Dead in Level 700'''
 As of '''29/7/1969''', an exploration agent from [[Apollo-∞]], James E. Robinson, has been found dead by another exploration team in Level 700. Footage was recovered from his camera, and a new phenomenon corresponding to the aforementioned level has been found inside it. Wanderers in or travelling to Level 700 are urged to be alert and updated to the information provided on said level. An update will be made to the Level 700 page in [[The Database]] going on detail about this phenomenon.

{{User:PayourTAXES769/sectionwarning|bad_content=descriptions of gore}}
  ✎ '''The Eclipse'''
{{Blurimage|file=The Eclipse.gif|align=left|caption=A recording of The Eclipse occurring. Photographer deceased.}} '''The Eclipse''' is a rare and hazardous event unique to Level 700, marked by an unidentified object eclipsing the Dusk Star. During this occurrence, the environment takes on a deep red hue, oxygen becomes hazardous, temperatures increase alarmingly, and all known exits become inaccessible. The Dusk Star's light becomes more lethal as the Eclipse progresses. Those exposed to the Eclipse are immediately affected by the Nightgaze phenomenon, with Almond Water no longer being effective. Footage data suggests victims remain conscious, experiencing full-body paralysis while fixated on the Dusk Star. Prolonged exposure causes progressive eye damage and severe burns that spread throughout the body in slow manner, melting skin and burning the victim's insides. Despite the severity, death does not occur until the Eclipse concludes. The damage starts with the burning of the retina, the melting of the ocular system and subsequently the rest of the body. Oxygen also becomes a hazard, with one case describing that "breathing felt like a burn in your throat and chest," suggesting oxygen gains hazardous properties that cause pain when inhaling it. The cause and timing of the Eclipse are unknown, making exiting the level as early as possible the only reliable way of avoidance. Unlike standard Nightgaze cases, bodies of Eclipse victims do not vanish but instead gradually erode into the lunar surface if left uncollected. Only one wanderer has been documented to have survived the Eclipse. This was achieved by hiding underground, as the only way to survive the Eclipse without exiting is to avoid exposure to the Dusk Star during the phenomenon, though oxygen danger still causes difficulties.  What's for sure is: Once all turns red, your last realization will be what ''truly'' painted the surface of the Bloodmoon.
{{Collapse|num=3|initial=
And then what remains?
|final=
And then what remains?
|body=
{{Background|file=Void.png}}
You.
Laying down on the grey wasteland beneath...
Just as you did back on earth.
Where once, you'd stare at the gleaming moon.
Now, you can only stare at the inky black that meets the eye.
Hoping, that maybe just once, instead..
You'd see home.
[[File:Earthrise1.jpg|center|350px|thumb|link= ]]
...
[[File:Earthdown.png|center|350px|thumb|link= ]]
But even now, that's but a small wish for a man. A faint, fragile—''weak'', hope.
And all you can do.. is watch.
And find beauty in the sheer irony of it all.
